1	package bead

3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"
6		"os"
7		"strings"
8		"testing"
9	
10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
12	)

14	// TestAttachmentClosingExternalizesEvents asserts that closing a bead moves
15	// its inline event history into a sidecar attachment under
16	// .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl and clears the inline copy from the row.
17	//
18	// Acceptance: AC1 (closed beads write events to the attachment store, not
19	// inline) and AC3 (bead-row size shrinks).
20	func TestAttachmentClosingExternalizesEvents(t *testing.T) {
21		s := newTestStore(t)
22	
23		b := &Bead{Title: "to close"}
24		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
25		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "looks good"}))
26		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "summary", Summary: "completed"}))
27	
28		// Capture the row size before close so we can demonstrate AC3 shrinkage.
29		beforeRowBytes := len(beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID))
30	
31		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
32	
33		// Sidecar exists.
34		attPath := s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID)
35		info, err := os.Stat(attPath)
36		require.NoError(t, err, "events.jsonl sidecar should exist after close")
37		assert.True(t, info.Size() > 0)
38	
39		// The on-disk row no longer carries inline events; it carries an
40		// attachment ref instead.
41		rowBytes := beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID)
42		assert.NotContains(t, string(rowBytes), `"events":`)
43		assert.Contains(t, string(rowBytes), `"`+EventsAttachmentExtraKey+`":`)
44	
45		// AC3: the row shrank.
46		assert.Less(t, len(rowBytes), beforeRowBytes,
47			"closed bead row should be smaller after externalization")
48	
49		// Sidecar carries both events with their bodies intact.
50		events, err := s.readEventsAttachment(b.ID)
51		require.NoError(t, err)
52		require.Len(t, events, 2)
53		assert.Equal(t, "review", events[0].Kind)
54		assert.Equal(t, "APPROVE", events[0].Summary)
55		assert.Equal(t, "looks good", events[0].Body)
56		assert.Equal(t, "summary", events[1].Kind)
57	}
58	
59	// TestAttachmentShowReadsTransparently exercises AC2: callers read events
60	// through the normal Events() API regardless of where they live, and the
61	// `--json` show projection inlines events into the marshaled object.
62	func TestAttachmentShowReadsTransparently(t *testing.T) {
63		s := newTestStore(t)
64	
65		b := &Bead{Title: "show me"}
66		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
67		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "model=opus"}))
68		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "rationale"}))
69		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
70	
71		// Events() returns the externalized log transparently.
72		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
73		require.NoError(t, err)
74		require.Len(t, events, 2)
75		assert.Equal(t, "routing", events[0].Kind)
76		assert.Equal(t, "review", events[1].Kind)
77	
78		// LoadEventsInline replays the events into Extra["events"] so the JSON
79		// show path projects a single uniform shape.
80		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
81		require.NoError(t, err)
82		require.NotNil(t, got)
83		require.NoError(t, s.LoadEventsInline(got))
84		raw, ok := got.Extra["events"]
85		require.True(t, ok, "LoadEventsInline must populate Extra[events]")
86		inlined := decodeBeadEvents(raw)
87		require.Len(t, inlined, 2)
88		_, attachStillSet := got.Extra[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
89		assert.False(t, attachStillSet, "in-memory inlining drops the attachment ref")
90	
91		// On-disk row was not modified by the inline operation.
92		rowBytes := beadRowBytes(t, s, b.ID)
93		assert.NotContains(t, string(rowBytes), `"events":`)
94		assert.Contains(t, string(rowBytes), `"`+EventsAttachmentExtraKey+`":`)
95	}
96	
97	// TestAttachmentExportInlinesEvents covers AC4: export emits a single inline
98	// `events` array per row so bd/br interchange survives.
99	func TestAttachmentExportInlinesEvents(t *testing.T) {
100		s := newTestStore(t)
101	
102		b := &Bead{Title: "export me"}
103		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
104		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "model=opus"}))
105		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "ok"}))
106		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
107	
108		var buf bytes.Buffer
109		require.NoError(t, s.ExportTo(&buf))
110	
111		out := strings.TrimSpace(buf.String())
112		require.NotEmpty(t, out)
113	
114		var row map[string]any
115		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &row))
116	
117		events, ok := row["events"].([]any)
118		require.True(t, ok, "export must include inline events array; got %v", row["events"])
119		require.Len(t, events, 2)
120		first := events[0].(map[string]any)
121		assert.Equal(t, "routing", first["kind"])
122	
123		// The attachment ref must NOT appear in exported JSON — bd/br doesn't
124		// know how to resolve it and would otherwise see a dangling pointer.
125		_, refStillThere := row[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
126		assert.False(t, refStillThere, "export must drop the attachment ref")
127	}
128	
129	// TestExportStreamsArchiveAndAttachmentEvents asserts that after `ddx bead
130	// migrate` moves closed beads into the beads-archive collection (with their
131	// events externalized to the attachment store), `ddx bead export` still
132	// streams a single JSONL document covering both the active and archived
133	// rows, with archived beads' events inlined back into the row so bd/br
134	// importers see no dangling attachment refs (ADR-004 compatibility guard #3
135	// for AC2/AC3/AC4).
136	func TestExportStreamsArchiveAndAttachmentEvents(t *testing.T) {
137		s := newTestStore(t)
138	
139		// Active open bead with no events — it must survive export.
140		open := &Bead{Title: "still open"}
141		require.NoError(t, s.Create(open))
142	
143		// A closed bead with inline events. Migrate will externalize then archive it.
144		closed := &Bead{Title: "to archive"}
145		require.NoError(t, s.Create(closed))
146		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(closed.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "model=opus"}))
147		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(closed.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "ship"}))
148		require.NoError(t, s.Close(closed.ID))
149	
150		stats, err := s.Migrate()
151		require.NoError(t, err)
152		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, stats.Archived, 1, "migrate should archive the closed bead")
153	
154		// Sanity: the closed bead is gone from the active collection.
155		activeBeads, err := s.ReadAll()
156		require.NoError(t, err)
157		for _, b := range activeBeads {
158			assert.NotEqual(t, closed.ID, b.ID, "archived bead must not remain in active store")
159		}
160	
161		var buf bytes.Buffer
162		require.NoError(t, s.ExportTo(&buf))
163	
164		out := strings.TrimSpace(buf.String())
165		lines := strings.Split(out, "\n")
166		require.Len(t, lines, 2, "export must include both the active and archived bead")
167	
168		rowsByID := make(map[string]map[string]any, 2)
169		for _, line := range lines {
170			var row map[string]any
171			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &row))
172			id, _ := row["id"].(string)
173			rowsByID[id] = row
174		}
175	
176		openRow, ok := rowsByID[open.ID]
177		require.True(t, ok, "active open bead must be in export")
178		assert.Equal(t, "open", openRow["status"])
179	
180		archivedRow, ok := rowsByID[closed.ID]
181		require.True(t, ok, "archived bead must be streamed inline alongside active rows")
182		assert.Equal(t, "closed", archivedRow["status"])
183	
184		// Events must be inlined back into the row — bd/br round-trip relies
185		// on this. The attachment ref must NOT survive the export.
186		events, ok := archivedRow["events"].([]any)
187		require.True(t, ok, "archived row must carry inline events; got %v", archivedRow["events"])
188		require.Len(t, events, 2)
189		assert.Equal(t, "routing", events[0].(map[string]any)["kind"])
190		assert.Equal(t, "review", events[1].(map[string]any)["kind"])
191		_, refStillThere := archivedRow[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
192		assert.False(t, refStillThere, "export must drop the attachment ref")
193	
194		// Round-trip: the export stream is consumable as bd-format JSONL —
195		// every line parses as a bead record carrying bd-compatible fields.
196		for _, line := range lines {
197			b, uerr := unmarshalBead([]byte(line))
198			require.NoError(t, uerr, "exported line must parse as a bd-compatible bead")
199			assert.NotEmpty(t, b.ID)
200			assert.NotEmpty(t, b.Status)
201		}
202	}
203	
204	// TestAttachmentAppendEventAfterCloseAppendsToSidecar covers a subtle case:
205	// once a bead is closed the inline path is gone, so a follow-up AppendEvent
206	// must extend the sidecar rather than re-introducing inline storage.
207	func TestAttachmentAppendEventAfterCloseAppendsToSidecar(t *testing.T) {
208		s := newTestStore(t)
209	
210		b := &Bead{Title: "post-close evt"}
211		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
212		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "first"}))
213		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
214	
215		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "audit", Summary: "post-close"}))
216	
217		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
218		require.NoError(t, err)
219		require.NotNil(t, got)
220		_, hasInline := got.Extra["events"]
221		assert.False(t, hasInline, "post-close append must not re-inline events")
222	
223		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
224		require.NoError(t, err)
225		require.Len(t, events, 2)
226		assert.Equal(t, "audit", events[1].Kind)
227		assert.Equal(t, "post-close", events[1].Summary)
228	}
229	
230	// TestAttachmentReopenInlinesEvents exercises Reopen's promise: a reopened
231	// bead returns to the active queue with its full event history back inline,
232	// the attachment ref cleared, and a fresh "reopen" event appended.
233	func TestAttachmentReopenInlinesEvents(t *testing.T) {
234		s := newTestStore(t)
235	
236		b := &Bead{Title: "reopen me"}
237		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
238		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "routing", Summary: "first"}))
239		require.NoError(t, s.AppendEvent(b.ID, BeadEvent{Kind: "review", Summary: "APPROVE", Body: "ok"}))
240		require.NoError(t, s.Close(b.ID))
241	
242		// Sanity: closed bead has the attachment.
243		require.FileExists(t, s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID))
244	
245		require.NoError(t, s.Reopen(b.ID, "needs more work", ""))
246	
247		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
248		require.NoError(t, err)
249		require.NotNil(t, got)
250		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)
251		_, attachStillSet := got.Extra[EventsAttachmentExtraKey]
252		assert.False(t, attachStillSet, "reopen must drop the attachment ref")
253	
254		events, err := s.Events(b.ID)
255		require.NoError(t, err)
256		require.Len(t, events, 3, "two prior events plus the reopen event")
257		assert.Equal(t, "reopen", events[2].Kind)
258		assert.Equal(t, "needs more work", events[2].Summary)
259	
260		// The sidecar file is removed once events are inlined back.
261		_, statErr := os.Stat(s.eventsAttachmentPath(b.ID))
262		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
263			"sidecar should be deleted after reopen inlines events")
264	}
265	
266	// TestAttachmentClosureGateAcceptsAttachedEvents guards against the gate
267	// rejecting a follow-up close on a bead whose events live in the sidecar
268	// (the gate's existing check only inspected Extra[events]).
269	func TestAttachmentClosureGateAcceptsAttachedEvents(t *testing.T) {
270		b := &Bead{
271			ID: "ddx-gate-att",
272			Extra: map[string]any{
273				EventsAttachmentExtraKey: "ddx-gate-att/events.jsonl",
274				"closing_commit_sha":     "deadbeef",
275			},
276		}
277		// No inline events, but the attachment ref + closing commit together
278		// must satisfy the gate (the inline-only path would otherwise reject).
279		assert.NoError(t, ClosureGate(b))
280	}
281	
282	// beadRowBytes returns the raw JSONL line for the given bead ID from the
283	// store's primary file, so tests can compare row sizes before/after
284	// externalization (AC3).
285	func beadRowBytes(t *testing.T, s *Store, id string) []byte {
286		t.Helper()
287		data, err := os.ReadFile(s.File)
288		require.NoError(t, err)
289		for _, line := range bytes.Split(data, []byte("\n")) {
290			line = bytes.TrimSpace(line)
291			if len(line) == 0 {
292				continue
293			}
294			var m map[string]any
295			if err := json.Unmarshal(line, &m); err != nil {
296				continue
297			}
298			if rid, _ := m["id"].(string); rid == id {
299				return line
300			}
301		}
302		t.Fatalf("bead %s not found in %s", id, s.File)
303		return nil
304	}

commit dcd29dd528e2a81a8192f78e9924ce04cf975b62
Author: Test User <test@example.com>
Date:   Sat May 2 00:17:21 2026 -0400

    feat(bead): externalize closed-bead events to attachment sidecar [ddx-5d67c121]
    
    Per ADR-004's attachment model and TD-027 §c, closing a bead now moves its
    inline event history into a sidecar at .ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl
    and replaces Extra["events"] with an Extra["events_attachment"] reference.
    The active row stays small while Events(), AppendEvent (post-close), and
    Reopen all read/write through the right physical store transparently.
    
    ddx bead show --json and Store.ExportTo re-inline the events array on
    the way out so bd/br interchange continues to see a single inline events
    shape; the on-disk row is unchanged.
    
    ClosureGate accepts attached events as evidence so re-closing an already-
    externalized bead does not regress the gate.

data.text: Committed. The sidecar attachment storage was already implemented in a prior bead (cli/internal/bead/attachments.go); this commit fills the explicit AC5 gap by adding a missing-sidecar fallback test (`TestAttachmentMissingSidecarFallback`) that verifies `readEventsAttachment`, `Events()`, and `LoadEventsInline` all tolerate a deleted sidecar without crashing.
